name: Yamnaya Culture
desc: Pastoral culture of the Pontic-Caspian steppe (3300–2600 BCE), known for kurgan burials and linked to Indo-European migrations.

name: Afonasiev Culture
desc: Early Bronze Age culture in southern Siberia (3300–2500 BCE), considered the easternmost branch of the Yamnaya horizon.

name: Sintashta Culture
desc: Bronze Age culture of the southern Urals (2200–1800 BCE), known for chariot burials and fortified settlements.

name: Seima-Turbino Phenomenon
desc: Transcultural bronze-working network across northern Eurasia (2000–1500 BCE), famous for advanced metalwork and wide geographic spread.

name: Andronovo Culture
desc: Bronze Age culture of Central Asia (2000–1450 BCE), associated with early Indo-Iranian peoples and metal production.
